articles.title: Critical cPanel Authentication Vulnerability Identified — Update Your Server Immediately
articles.summary: cPanel has released  security updates to address a security issue impacting various authentication paths that could allow an attacker to obtain access to the control panel software.  The problem affects all currently supported versions, according to an alert released by cPanel on Tuesday. The issue has been addressed in the following versions -   11.110.0.97  11.118.0.63  11.126.0.54  11.132.0.29  11.136.0.5  11.134.0.20    "If your server is not running a supported version of cPanel that is eligible for this update, it is highly recommended that you work toward updating your server as soon as possible, as it may also be affected," cPanel noted.  While cPanel did not share any details about the vulnerability, web hosting and domain registration company Namecheap disclosed  that it "relates to an authentication login exploit that could allow unauthorized access to the control panel."  As a precautionary measure, the company has applied a firewall rule to block access...
articles.title_fr: Vulnérabilité critique d'authentification cPanel identifiée — Mettez à jour votre serveur immédiatement
articles.summary_fr: cPanel a publié des mises à jour de sécurité pour corriger une vulnérabilité critique affectant diverses voies d'authentification, pouvant permettre à un attaquant d'accéder au logiciel du panneau de contrôle. Cette faille concerne toutes les versions actuellement prises en charge, et il est fortement recommandé de mettre à jour les serveurs dès que possible. La société Namecheap a précisé que l'exploit concerne une faille de connexion d'authentification permettant un accès non autorisé.

articles.title: CISA Adds Actively Exploited ConnectWise and Windows Flaws to KEV
articles.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday added  two security flaws impacting ConnectWise ScreenConnect and Microsoft Windows to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities ( KEV ) catalog, based on evidence of active exploitation.  The vulnerabilities are listed below -   CVE-2024-1708  (CVSS score: 8.4) - A path traversal vulnerability in  ConnectWise ScreenConnect that could allow an attacker to execute remote code or directly impact confidential data and critical systems. (Fixed in February 2024)  CVE-2026-32202  (CVSS score: 4.3) - A protection mechanism failure vulnerability in  Microsoft Windows Shell that could allow an unauthorized attacker to perform spoofing over a network. (Fixed in April 2026)    The addition of CVE-2026-32202 to the KEV catalog comes a day after Microsoft updated its advisory  for the flaw to acknowledge it had come under active exploitation.  Although Microsoft has not disclosed the nature of the attacks weaponizi...
articles.title_fr: La CISA ajoute des failles ConnectWise et Windows activement exploitées à son catalogue KEV
articles.summary_fr: L'agence américaine CISA a ajouté deux failles de sécurité, affectant ConnectWise ScreenConnect et Microsoft Windows, à son catalogue des vulnérabilités connues et exploitées (KEV). Ces vulnérabilités, CVE-2024-1708 et CVE-2026-32202, sont activement exploitées et peuvent permettre l'exécution de code à distance ou l'usurpation d'identité. L'ajout de la faille Windows fait suite à la confirmation par Microsoft de son exploitation active.

articles.title: LiteLLM CVE-2026-42208 SQL Injection Exploited within 36 Hours of Disclosure
articles.summary: In yet another instance  of threat actors quickly jumping on the exploitation bandwagon, a newly disclosed critical security flaw in BerriAI's LiteLLM  Python package has come under active exploitation in the wild within 36 hours of the bug becoming public knowledge.  The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-42208 (CVSS score: 9.3), is an SQL injection that could be exploited to modify the underlying LiteLLM proxy database.  "A database query used during proxy API key checks mixed the caller-supplied key value into the query text instead of passing it as a separate parameter," LiteLLM maintainers said  in an alert last week.   "An unauthenticated attacker could send a specially crafted Authorization header to any LLM API route (for example, POST /chat/completions) and reach this query through the proxy's error-handling path. An attacker could read data from the proxy's database and may be able to modify it, leading to unauthorized access to the proxy and the c...
articles.title_fr: LiteLLM CVE-2026-42208 : Injection SQL exploitée dans les 36 heures suivant sa divulgation
articles.summary_fr: Une faille de sécurité critique, CVE-2026-42208, dans le package Python LiteLLM de BerriAI a été activement exploitée moins de 36 heures après sa divulgation publique. Cette vulnérabilité d'injection SQL permet à un attaquant non authentifié de modifier la base de données proxy sous-jacente de LiteLLM. En envoyant un en-tête d'autorisation spécialement conçu, un attaquant peut lire et potentiellement modifier les données de la base de données, entraînant un accès non autorisé.

script:
Bonjour et bienvenue dans votre Flash Info Cyber du jour, le rendez-vous incontournable de la sécurité numérique !

Commençons avec une alerte majeure pour les administrateurs de serveurs : une vulnérabilité critique
